title: Reading List
text:
I’ve been inspired by the recent trend of people posting their reading lists so I’m joining in on the fun. The plan is to read (at least) one book a month and keep that going as long as I can.
Note: This is a no research paper zone, just leisure reading :)

title: Reading List
text:

title: Curriculum Vitae or Resume Format 1
text:
Just as with a standard cv or resume, use whatever headings and format makes the most sense for you. These sections are just examples. See here for an overview of markdown formatting in quarto.
For the mainfont: pdf option in the header, fonts that work will generally be in ~/Library/Fonts (MacOS) or C:\Windows\Fonts (Windows). The pdf is rendered by the site creator so any font found on the site creator’s computer can be used. See also https://quarto.org/docs/reference/formats/pdf.html#fonts .

title: Name
text:
This is a template for an academic personal website. It was created with grad students and postdocs in mind, however, others may find it a helpful template as well! We’re hopeful that it will be relevant to those seeking industry as well as academic positions and have created page options that we think are more industry-relevant, however we are more familiar with academic websites. The github repository is https://github.com/ngalanter/academic-website-template.
This template uses quarto, you can learn more about quarto websites here: https://quarto.org/docs/websites.
This page uses the about page template, see details here: https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-about.html. This page can be used to give an overview of yourself, and you can link externally and internally.
This template uses the minty theme with slight color customization, to change the theme or create a custom theme go to the _quarto.yml file. See here for more details about themes and see here for a preview of what the pre-made themes generally look like.
See the repository readme for more information about using this template and publishing via quarto and github pages.
This goal of this template is to provide several types of pages that might be relevant, but most personal websites don’t have all of these pages!

News
Published paper Paper 3 in Journal 3
Released preprint Paper 4
Exciting update here
Presented poster Poster at Conference
Published paper Paper 2 in Journal 2

title: Ameer Dharamshi
text:
About Me
I am a fourth-year PhD Candidate in the Department of Biostatistics at the University of Washington supervised by Professors Daniela Witten and Jon Wakefield. In August 2026 I will be joining the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at McGill University as an Assistant Professor. My research focuses on developing strategies for selective inference and predictive model uncertainty quantification that are applicable in complex data settings and/or with small sample sizes. I also actively work on collaborative projects in demography, global development, and quantitative social science that aim to estimate various population indicators (e.g., mortality, fertility, and school completion rates) at national and subnational levels.
Previously, I worked as a research and data consultant for the Global Education Monitoring Report at UNESCO. I completed my Master of Science in Statistical Sciences at the University of Toronto and my Bachelor of Mathematics at the University of Waterloo.
For more about my academic and professional experiences, please see my Research and CV. I am always looking for interesting collaborations so if you have a project in mind, please do feel free to reach out!
Outside of my work, I like to ice skate, play squash, run, bake, and really enjoy a good book.
